articles.title: 5 takeaways from Patriots’ first 2026 OTA practice
articles.description:
"[Vrabel is] ready to roll. He ain’t changed one bit.”

The post 5 takeaways from Patriots’ first 2026 OTA practice appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots held their first OTA practice on May 27, 2026, at Gillette Stadium.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasized readiness, indicating a strong team mentality this season.
Local fans can expect an energized team as they prepare for the upcoming season.

articles.title: Minneapolis police chief resigns after interfering with an investigation, mayor says
articles.description:
The mayor of Minneapolis says the police chief hired to oversee reforms after George Floyd’s killing has chosen to resign rather than face discipline for interfering with an investigation into his conduct.

The post Minneapolis police chief resigns after interfering with an investigation, mayor says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Minneapolis police chief resigns amid investigation interference, impacting police reform efforts.
Mayor states the chief chose resignation over facing disciplinary action for misconduct.
Boston residents may see increased scrutiny on local police leadership and accountability measures.

articles.title: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel focused on spring workouts after missing final day of draft last month
articles.description:
“My family is great. I love (his wife) Jen (Vrabel). I love the boys. I love my personal friends,” he said. “And then, this spring is focused on really the coaching staff, the players, more importantly than the coaching staff.”

The post Patriots coach Mike Vrabel focused on spring workouts after missing final day of draft last month appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes spring workouts for team development.
Vrabel missed the final day of the draft last month.
Focus on coaching staff and players aims to enhance team performance for Boston fans.

articles.title: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close
articles.description:
The longtime Hemenway Street restaurant will serve its final customers Thursday night.

The post Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Woody's Grill & Tap on Hemenway Street will close permanently on Thursday night.
The restaurant has been a staple in Fenway for many years.
Local residents will lose a popular dining option in the Fenway area.
